title: A signed-direction invariant for triangle tilings, and the exclusion of primes congruent to 3 modulo 4
description: A translation-invariant, signed-direction tiling functional that, within the 2pi/3 branch of Laczkovich's classification of triangle tilings, proves that no prime number of 2pi/3 tiles tiles an isosceles triangle (for every prime, where only a finite lower bound was previously known and Zhang's constructions had led to the conjecture that no prime occurs). With a self-contained reduction of the scalene shapes, this completes the exclusion of primes congruent to 3 modulo 4 as numbers of congruent triangles into which a triangle can be cut, conditional on the cited Laczkovich-Beeson classification; in particular no triangle can be cut into 19 congruent triangles (Erdos Problem 634). Consequently the prime case of the full problem is complete: a prime p occurs iff p = 2, p = 3, or p ≡ 1 (mod 4). The paper also determines the admissible spectrum of each sporadic 2pi/3 branch (isosceles target: N = d·w²·(a+2b) with e | w(c−a−b) and the parity refinement M ≡ N mod 2), settles every previously undetermined value up to 62 (no triangle can be cut into 14, 15, 21, 22, 30, 33, 35, 38, 39, 42, 51, 55, 56, 57, 60, or 62 congruent triangles; and A TRIANGLE CAN BE CUT INTO 44 CONGRUENT TRIANGLES - the isosceles (16,16,22) into 44 copies of the (2,3,4) tile, found by exhaustive search and verified exactly, the first known tiling in the isosceles 3a+2b=pi cases and the smallest known tiling in any incommensurable branch) via a complete branch sweep plus an exhaustive exact search of the surviving finite instances, excludes N = 46 (the sharpest test of Zhang's completeness conjecture, in agreement with that conjecture), proves the spectrum theorem (the counts passing all invariant conditions on the isosceles and F1 targets are exactly Zhang's constructed families - the necessary side of these branches is closed), reduces the equilateral square criteria to elementary divisor conditions on 16N^2, and proves that membership in the tile-count set is decidable. Includes the paper, machine-verification code (Python, exact arithmetic), and a Lean 4 + Mathlib formalization of the number-theoretic core (axiom-clean, no sorry). The geometric lemmas are verified numerically and are offered for refereeing. Developed with AI assistance (Anthropic Claude); presented for peer review.
